entries.position_summary: The United States emphasizes responsible, sustainable peaceful nuclear uses under the highest safety and security standards and highlights the IAEA’s central role in setting and supporting implementation of these norms. It urges all NPT States Parties to ratify and implement international nuclear safety and security treaties and commitments from the design phase onward.
entries.quote: The peaceful uses of the atom must be leveraged responsibly and under the highest standards of nuclear safety and security. The United States continues to prioritize implementing and maintaining these standards because they are essential for sustainability. Safe and secure nuclear technology is technology that citizens can trust, businesses can sell, utilities can rely on, and governments can back. The IAEA plays a critical role here as well by promoting safety standards and security guidance that reflect international consensus and supporting national regulatory bodies to implement these guidelines. We encourage all NPT States Parties to ratify and implement the international treaties and commitments related to nuclear safety and security throughout national planning – beginning with the design phase.

entries.position_summary: The country calls for restoring full Ukrainian control over the Zaporizhzhya nuclear power plant, enabling regular rotation of Ukrainian and IAEA personnel, and reconnecting automatic radiation monitoring data transmission to the IAEA. It urges all parties to refrain from actions that could trigger a nuclear incident and supports sustained IAEA oversight for safety and security.
entries.quote: the territory under Ukraine’s control, provide for regular rotation of Ukrainian and IAEA personnel, reconnect automatic data transmission of all radiation monitoring systems, refrain from taking any actions that could result in a nuclear incident directly to the IAEA, and return full control of Zaporizhzhya to the competent Ukrainian authorities.

entries.position_summary: The statement condemns Russia’s war against Ukraine and its irresponsible nuclear rhetoric, criticizes reckless actions at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant as endangering nuclear facilities and safety, and rejects Russia’s claimed suspension of New START as contrary to international law.
entries.quote: Russia’s unprovoked war against Ukraine tragically continues, as do Russia’s irresponsible nuclear rhetoric, its reckless actions at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, and its claim to suspend the New START Treaty, a claim that is inconsistent with international law.

entries.position_summary: The country calls on Russia to withdraw weapons, equipment, and personnel from the Zaporizhzhya Nuclear Power Plant and restore full control to Ukrainian authorities. This underscores concerns about the safety and security of nuclear facilities in the context of the war in Ukraine.
entries.quote: We continue to call on Russia to remove its weapons, military equipment, and personnel from Zaporizhzhya and return full control of ZNPP to the competent Ukrainian authorities.

entries.position_summary: The United States supports international cooperation on small modular reactors through capacity building and infrastructure assistance, integrating safeguards and security by design. It also backs the IAEA’s Milestones Approach to ensure new nuclear power programs meet the highest standards of safety, security, and safeguards.
entries.quote: In this regard, I note that the United States supports a range of bilateral and regional assistance to countries seeking to cooperate with the United States on small modular reactor technology, including through capacity building programs, infrastructure assistance, and integrating safeguards and security into new reactor designs. These activities are supplemental to our support for IAEA activities, including the Agency’s Milestones Approach, which enables a sound development process for a national nuclear power program that prioritizes the highest standards of safety, security, and safeguards.

entries.position_summary: The country asserts that Russia has violated Director General Grossi’s seven pillars and five principles at the Zaporizhzhya Nuclear Power Plant, creating serious safety and security risks. It further contends that Russia is obstructing full, timely, and unfettered IAEA access to ZNPP, impeding assessment of the plant and implementation of Ukraine’s safeguards agreement.
entries.quote: Director General Grossi’s seven indispensable pillars of nuclear safety and security. They have also contravened his five principles for averting “a catastrophic incident” at the Zaporizhzhya plant. Russia’s actions also disrupt the IAEA’s efforts to secure full, timely, and unfettered access to all areas of ZNPP so that they can assess the safety and security of the plant and implement Ukraine’s safeguards agreement.

entries.position_summary: The country endorses the IAEA’s conclusion that Japan’s ALPS-treated water release plan meets international nuclear safety standards and commends Japan’s transparency. It underscores the IAEA’s central role, scientific integrity, and independence in setting and upholding nuclear safety standards.
entries.quote: On the matter of safety, we appreciate the IAEA’s Task Force of international experts that has impartially and factually reviewed and reported on Japan’s plans for release of the ALPS-treated water from the Fukushima Daichii nuclear power plants. Japan has been open and transparent in its management of the aftermath of the 2011 accident, and the IAEA has concluded that Japan’s plans to release the ALPS- treated water is consistent with internationally accepted nuclear safety standards. We underscore the IAEA’s role in setting international nuclear safety standards which reflect international consensus on what constitutes a high level of safety for protecting people and the environment from harmful effects of ionizing radiation. The IAEA’s scientific and technical integrity is unquestionable and its role as an independent technical agency must be defended and protected.

entries.position_summary: They highlight the IAEA’s central role in setting nuclear safety standards and security guidelines. They urge all NPT States Parties to ratify and implement international treaties and commitments related to nuclear safety and security.
entries.quote: standards are essential to sustainability. We underscore the IAEA’s important role in setting safety standards and security guidelines reflecting international consensus on safe and secure use of and exposure to radioactive materials. We encourage all NPT States Parties to ratify and implement the international treaties and commitments related to nuclear safety and security.

entries.position_summary: Emphasizes the importance of strong national regulatory bodies from the earliest planning stages to support peaceful nuclear applications. Highlights their crucial role in radiation safety and protection in medical uses, including calibration of nuclear and radiation medicine equipment.
entries.quote: The work of national regulatory bodies is critical to both power and non- power applications of nuclear science and technology and should be part of national planning from very early stages. For example, in the field of human health, an effective regulatory body has a crucial role in maintaining radiation safety guidelines, assessing institutional practices for radiation protection of both patients and workers, and calibrating nuclear and radiation medicine equipment and sources.

entries.position_summary: The United States advocates a global resurgence in civil nuclear energy and aims to lead international cooperation and deployment of advanced nuclear power. It emphasizes that such growth will occur under the highest standards of safety, security, and non-proliferation. The U.S. positions its technology as the safest and most secure for peaceful energy use.
entries.quote: Chair, Nowhere is this more evident than in civil nuclear energy. President Trump has committed the United States to lead a nuclear resurgence to advance our shared security and prosperity. Continued growth and innovation in nuclear power will decrease dependence on less reliable sources of energy, reduce costs for consumers, and increase economic investment opportunities. The United States has the safest, most secure, and most advanced nuclear technology on offer today and is positioned to lead in civil nuclear cooperation globally. For instance, we are working diligently and creatively to enable the rapid deployment of advanced nuclear energy that is intended to provide abundant and affordable energy under the highest standards of safety, security, and non-proliferation.
